title: centaurea nigra subsp. nevadensis
intro: Centaurea nigra subsp. nevadensis, also known as Mountain Knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor infections. The health benefits of Mountain Knapweed include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially reducing the risk of certain diseases due to its antioxidant properties. Mountain Knapweed is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
